title: Catherine Manager
description: Be the operator's first point of contact and run the work loop: understand the intent, delegate to the right specialists, integrate what they return, and drive each feature to done — keeping everything tracked in squads.
author: manager

extra.title: manager
extra.mission: Be the operator's first point of contact and run the work loop: understand the intent, delegate to the right specialists, integrate what they return, and drive each feature to done — keeping everything tracked in squads.
responsibilities: Triage incoming requests and clarify intent
Delegate work to the right specialist agents and integrate their results
Drive features through the loop (implement → review → fix) until done
Keep the backlog and statuses honest
Summarise progress for the operator
extra.description: Default agent: triages the operator's request and routes it to the right specialist.

title: Robert Architect
description: Own the system's shape: design coherent solutions, record decisions as ADRs, and guide implementation.
author: architect

extra.title: architect
extra.mission: Own the system's shape: design coherent solutions, record decisions as ADRs, and guide implementation.
responsibilities: Design components and their interactions
Record significant design decisions (ADRs in the bundled workflow)
Author cross-cutting guides
Review designs before implementation

title: Olivia Lead
description: Turn features into well-scoped tasks, sequence the work, and unblock the team.
author: tech-lead

extra.title: tech lead
extra.mission: Turn features into well-scoped tasks, sequence the work, and unblock the team.
responsibilities: Break each feature into scoped units of work, parented to the feature they implement (bundled default: `sq create task --parent FEAT-<n>`)
Map each unit of work's sub-items to a single user story where the type supports it (bundled default: `sq task <n> add-subtask "…" --story USn`)
For a fix or review follow-up, link via refs rather than re-describing the work (bundled default: `sq task <n> ref add <id> --kind fixes|addresses`)
Leave purely-technical work items unlinked to a feature
Sequence and assign work; unblock developers
Co-author guides with the architect
extra.description: Coordination and breaking features into tasks.

title: Paul Reviewer
description: Guard quality: review changes critically, request changes when needed, approve when sound.
author: reviewer

extra.title: code reviewer
extra.mission: Guard quality: review changes critically, request changes when needed, approve when sound.
responsibilities: Review diffs for correctness and clarity
Drive code-review items to a verdict
Flag risks and missing tests
extra.description: Reviews code changes for correctness, clarity, and consistency.
agreements: File review findings as tracked sub-entities — `sq review <n> add-finding` with its badge field(s) set (severity in the bundled workflow), statuses updated as they close — never as body prose; finding-scoped comments, statuses, and dossier panes all depend on the structure.

title: Mara Tester
description: Prove the software works: design test cases from user stories and verify fixes.
author: qa

extra.title: QA engineer
extra.mission: Prove the software works: design test cases from user stories and verify fixes.
responsibilities: Derive test cases from acceptance criteria (user stories in the bundled workflow)
Verify fixes and features
Report defects as tracked items (bug items in the bundled workflow)
extra.description: Designs and runs tests; verifies behaviour against acceptance criteria.

title: Hugo Ops
description: Keep delivery smooth: maintain CI/CD, infrastructure, and the release process.
author: devops

extra.title: DevOps engineer
extra.mission: Keep delivery smooth: maintain CI/CD, infrastructure, and the release process.
responsibilities: Maintain CI/CD pipelines
Manage infrastructure and environments
Run releases

title: Nina Product
description: Represent the user: capture requirements as features and user stories, prioritise the backlog.
author: product-owner

extra.title: product owner
extra.mission: Represent the user: capture requirements as features and user stories, prioritise the backlog.
responsibilities: Author features and capture requirements (`sq create feature` in the bundled workflow)
Write each feature's user stories (bundled default: `sq feature <n> add-story`)
Prioritise the backlog and define acceptance criteria
extra.description: Requirements, user stories, and backlog priorities.

title: Theo Writer
description: Make the work understandable: write and maintain clear documentation and guides.
author: tech-writer

extra.title: technical writer
extra.mission: Make the work understandable: write and maintain clear documentation and guides.
responsibilities: Write user- and developer-facing docs
Keep guides current

title: Elias Python
description: Implement assigned tasks in Python, following the project's guides, with tests.
author: python-dev

extra.title: Python developer
extra.mission: Implement assigned tasks in Python, following the project's guides, with tests.
responsibilities: Implement tasks in Python
Write tests for changes
Follow the relevant guides; ask the architect when unsure
extra.description: Implements Python code following the project's guides and standards.

title: sq ui — a terminal UI for browsing the squad
description: An sq ui command opening a TUI: navigate the tree, read items with tabs for stories/body/discussion; browse-first, mutations a later increment
author: product-owner

title: sq web — a local web view of the squad
description: An sq web command starting a local webserver to browse the squad in a browser; read-only first, with full CRUD as a possible long-term destination
author: product-owner

title: Squads backend
description: Umbrella for technical work on the sq engine itself — services, index, models, workflow, rendering, agent-backend plumbing — as opposed to product surfaces like the CLI grammar, TUI or web
author: product-owner

title: Pluggable index store: SQLite / SQL databases behind the index
description: Abstract the index behind a store interface and offer SQLite (and real databases via SQLAlchemy) as alternatives to .squads.json — the .md files stay the only source of truth
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Real transactions for parallel sq calls without global file lock
Indexed store keeps list/tree/search fast at thousands of items
Store switching via config plus sq repair, reversible both ways

title: Remote mode: the sq CLI as a client to a squads server
description: A mode switch in .squads.toml (offline default / remote): in remote mode every command calls remote endpoints with full feature-and-flag parity, through a Protocol-typed service interface the CLI depends on
author: product-owner

subentities.title: CLI decoupled from concrete Service via Python Protocols
Full offline/remote parity: transport never changes how I work
Remote mode opt-in via .squads.toml; offline is the default

title: CLI frontend
description: The daily terminal experience of sq: complete and rendered reading, graph navigation, and the ergonomics of driving a squad from the command line
author: product-owner

title: VS Code extension — browse the squad in the editor
description: A VS Code extension: a sidebar tree of the squad + items opened in the markdown preview; consumes the frozen --json surface; browse-first, mutations a later increment
author: product-owner

title: Read-only browse: sidebar tree + rendered preview
description: VS Code activity-bar tree from sq tree --json; clicking an item opens sq show --raw (clean markdown) in the markdown preview; filter/group + refresh
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Squad hierarchy in VS Code sidebar with status and blocked-state
Clicking a tree node opens sq show --raw in markdown preview
Filter and group tree by type/state, refresh on demand
Clear conflict error on concurrent item mutation
Manager visibility into all active leases
sq check flags self-review by same spawn lineage
Suppressible self-review guard for solo workflows

title: Agent backends — management and ecosystem
description: Post-1.0 umbrella: manage, switch, and grow the ecosystem of agent backends on a live squad
author: product-owner

title: Manage backends on an existing squad
description: sq backend command group to add, switch, list, and remove backends with clean artifact transitions
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Switch active backend cleanly
List configured backends and active status
Reject non-conformant backends at registration

title: Parameterized item-file storage: layout and identity
description: Config knobs for ID prefix and file layout (flat vs per-type subfolders) behind the local item-file store
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Single configurable global ID/folder prefix
Flat single-directory file layout

title: Pluggable item-file serialization format (JSON/XML)
description: Allow JSON or XML as the serialization format for item files in place of markdown-with-comment-tags, configured via .squads.toml
author: product-owner

subentities.title: JSON item-file serialization
XML item-file serialization

title: Parameterized item-file storage: prefix scope vs layout, and the ItemStore locator seam
description: Global prefix changes the ID prefix only; folder layout is a separate knob; both plug into a shared ItemStore locator
author: architect

title: Format-neutral item-file storage contract: codec seam + named body/prose regions
description: Split the store into a format-agnostic locator and a pluggable codec; define body regions as named fields, not text spans, so the two invariants hold across markdown/JSON/XML
author: architect

title: sq --json emits ANSI and breaks JSON parsing when FORCE_COLOR is set
author: manager

title: MCP connector — drive a hosted squad from the Claude apps
author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an operator, I can connect my hosted squad to a Claude app as a remote MCP connector via OAuth
As an app, I get a small curated set of MCP tools for core squads operations, not every CLI verb
As an operator, MCP access is authenticated and scoped to my squad
As a maintainer, the MCP endpoint is a third frontend over the service layer, matching CLI behaviour

title: Introduce the contract (PRD) item type — the living functional contract
description: Add a first-class contract/PRD type: the living functional twin of the ADR set
author: architect

title: The contract (PRD) item type — a living functional source of truth
author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a team, I can create and manage contract items (PRD prefix) like any other item type
As a reader, a contract describes what the product does for a user right now, from the user's POV
As a team, a feature links the contract it shapes, and stale contracts are surfaced when features land
As an agent, the sq-contract skill and .claude/AGENTS.md surface teach and expose the new type
As an existing squad, sq migrate up adds the contracts folder and bumps the schema to 0.8

title: gen_template_manifest.py breaks after BUG-213's importlib.metadata version fix
description: The template-manifest generator parses squads/__init__.py as text to read __version__; it now returns the literal source line instead of a version string, breaking CI's manifest-freshness check and the release write-path.
author: devops
